items.title: Labor's warning over 'timing' of Mount Barker, Murray Bridge commitments

children.content: The Malinauskas government made $300 million in election promises for the cities of Mount Barker and Murray Bridge, but when they will be delivered remains uncertain after the two cities did not elect Labor MPs.

gallery.altText: A woman wearing red and a man in a suit look at a red sign which reads: Labor's plan for Murray Bridge

thumbnail.altText: A woman wearing red and a man in a suit look at a red sign which reads: Labor's plan for Murray Bridge

items.title: Teen accused of toilet block assault took 'calculated steps', court hears

children.content: Prosecutors say the allegations against a teenage boy accused of sexually assaulting a child in a toilet block are so serious he should face trial in an adult court.

image.altText: A street sign that sayd "Regano Rd", with another reading "Toilet" below it.

items.title: AFL apologises after Butters appeal upheld, umpire abuse conviction quashed

children.content: The AFL appeals board has quashed Zak Butters's conviction for abusive language against umpire Nick Foot, saying a Tribunal member listening in while driving his car for part of the original hearing constitutes a miscarriage of justice.

image.altText: AFL star midfielder Zak Butters.

items.title: Potato company fined $360,000 after propeller 'severely damaged' employee's arm

children.content: A Southern Mallee Potato company has been convicted and fined $360,000 after a plane propeller struck an employee's arm, leaving him with multiple injuries, including partial finger amputations.

image.altText: A crate of potato's on a farm.

items.title: Blood donation eligibility expands for gay and bisexual men, transgender people

children.content: From today, Australian Red Cross Lifeblood will use gender-neutral assessments for blood and platelets donations.

image.altText: a man sits in a reclinable chair with a bandage on his inner elbow and he squeezes a stress ball

items.title: Wine grape grower bakes cupcakes to pay bills as fuel costs bite in 'worst' harvest

children.content: Farmers in the midst of harvest in South Australia are having to get creative to help make ends meet.

gallery.altText: A fair-skinned woman in a red floral dress holds a cupcake on a glass plate in a kitchen.

thumbnail.altText: A fair-skinned woman in a red floral dress holds a cupcake on a glass plate in a kitchen.

items.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate's renewed defamation claim names Australian YouTuber

children.content: An Adelaide YouTuber has been identified in a renewed defamation lawsuit self-described misogynist Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan have lodged with a US court against some of their online critics.

image.altText: Andrew Tate wearing sunglasses walks with brother Tristan

items.title: Octopus species mysteriously disappears from Spencer Gulf

children.content: The once-abundant pale octopus has not been caught in lower Spencer Gulf for more than a year, and what happened to the species remains a mystery.

image.altText: An orange octopus on a blue fishing bin lip

items.title: 'No rush': Crows won't put timeline on Dawson's return to play

children.content: Adelaide coach Matthew Nicks says there will be no rush for captain Jordan Dawson to return to playing after the death of his brother last week.

image.altText: The Adelaide Oval big screen displays a picture of the Dawson family with a message of support as the crowd applauds

items.title: A blunder in Narungga leaves SA's electoral authority in damage control

children.content: The 2026 state election has been a reputational disaster for South Australia's electoral authority. Its blunder in Narungga was the "cherry on top".

image.altText: A hand puts a lower house vote into a sealed ballot box as another person points to the top of the box

items.title: Think you have ADHD? Here's what getting assessed looks like

children.content: Who can diagnose ADHD, what a proper assessment should include, and the red flags to look out for.

articles.title: The neighbourhoods where ADHD rates are skyrocketing

children.content: ADHD diagnoses in adults have exploded across Australia since 2017. For the first time, we have the data that shows where it is — and isn't — happening.

image.altText: A search bar saying "Enter your area" is superimposed on a map of Australia against a blue-tinged backdrop of medication.

gallery.altText: A close up of a person marking a page on a clipboard, sitting across from a person clasping their hands.

items.title: Prospector finds 800,000 year old australite

children.content: The object formed when a meteorite slammed into Earth more than 800,000 years ago.

image.altText: a small, round, black, slightly pitted rock held between man's fingers

items.title: Former footballer Barry Cable found not guilty of sexually abusing child

children.content: Former Australian Rules footballer Barry Cable has been found not guilty of sexually abusing a child in Perth in the 1960s.

image.altText: Barry Cable walks up the stairs to court followed by a media pack.

items.title: Chinese humanoid robot beats human half-marathon world record

children.content: The winner from a Chinese smartphone maker reportedly completed the 21-kilometre race in 50 minutes and 26 seconds.

image.altText: Humanoid robot runs a marathon track in Beijing.

items.title: Rebel Wilson accused of defaming actor with sexual harassment claim

children.content: Hollywood star Rebel Wilson is being sued in the Federal Court for defamation by one of the lead actor's in her directorial debut film, The Deb.

image.altText: Rebel Wilson arrives at the Federal Court of Australia

items.title: Inquest hears of mother's final moments before death in Darwin safe house

children.content: An inquest has heard harrowing details of the final moments of a 33-year-old mother who bled to death
after harming herself inside a Darwin domestic violence shelter.

image.altText: A large white sign showing a coat of arms and the words "local court".

items.title: 'This is not the way I'm going to go': Diver lost at sea details rescue

children.content: Stu Fillman was rescued alongside his dive instructor after being carried away from a dive company boat in rough swell.

image.altText: Mr Fillman has been left with lacerations to his neck.

items.title: Qld minister says he never broke law amid questions on voting registration

children.content: Queensland's Olympics Minister Tim Mander has insisted he always complied with the state's electoral rules after he enrolled to vote at the address of a staff member.

image.altText: Tim Mander talking and gesticulating to underscore his point

items.title: Tempted to order in? Try these quick recipes instead

children.content: From frittata to one-pot pasta, these speedy dinners are perfect for busy evenings and worth adding to your regular rotation.

items.title: 'I had no-one to talk to': The 92yo trying to save his dying language

children.content: Peter is the last living speaker of the First Nations language Thiinma. When he met linguist Rosie, they used music to help keep his knowledge alive, and formed a beautiful friendship along the way.

image.altText: Peter stands on stage at a microphone with bright lights behind him, wearing an akubra, shirt and bolo tie

image.altText: Elijah Hollands follows through after kicking a ball at Carlton training

items.title: Fishers make 'surprising' discovery of artificial leg on the beach

children.content: The prosthetic leg has been handed to police after being discovered by a couple while they were fishing on a NSW Mid North Coast beach.

image.altText: prosthetic leg pictured on the beach

items.title: Childcare worker pleads not guilty to rape, indecent treatment

children.content: Childcare worker Joshua James Capps has pleaded not guilty to three counts of indecent treatment of a child in his care, and one count of rape.

image.altText: Childrens toys including a giraffe inside a car and an abacus.

items.title: Australia faced the same dilemma as China in 2004 but made the wrong call

children.content: While the rest of us count the cost in higher fuel costs, higher inflation and a weaker economy,  China — the actual winner of the war — will be enjoying a surge of global interest in its solar panels, wind turbines and electric vehicles.

image.altText: A man in a red hard-hat stands in a field of massive solar panels speaking to someone out of frame

items.title: 'Hard to break the habit': Why free public transport isn't enough to shift from cars

children.content: Free public transport is a popular policy, but researchers say it's not enough to convince drivers to leave the car at home.

gallery.altText: A train on a city railway.

items.title: 'Australia's most expensive soccer pitch' project blows out to $20m

children.content: Five years ago the conversion of a washout-prone natural field to synthetic should've cost $3.3 million. Project changes and a legal dispute with the old contractor has blown out the price.

image.altText: a football clubhouse surrounded by temporary fencing

items.title: Woman tells jury she lied after allegedly seeing mum prepare fatal smoothie

children.content: Maree Mavis Crabtree is fighting three charges at a trial in the Supreme Court in Brisbane, including the murder and attempted murder of her son Jonathan Crabtree.

items.title: Australia's biggest AI factory planned for remote WA

children.content: A huge artificial intelligence data centre capable of training models like ChatGPT is planned for WA's remote north at a cost of billons of dollars.

items.title: Grieving mother of Belgian backpacker warns hikers not to trust phone maps

children.content: Backpacker Celine Cremer set off on a short walk with only a phone to guide her — and vanished. More than two years later, her family has an urgent message for other travellers.

image.altText: Ariane Mathieu, Celine Cremer's mother sits in a home space wearing a red sweater, a serious facial expression

items.title: Thousands displaced as fire destroys homes in East Malaysian state of Sabah

children.content: Thousands of people are displaced after a fire destroys about 1,000 homes in a coastal village in the East Malaysian state of Sabah, the fire department says.

image.altText: A thick tower of black smoke rises from a water village

gallery.altText: piles of rubble and sheets of corrugated iron.
